hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 34.8%, the crowd is pricing Arizona as a top-8 tournament contender, which feels about right for a program with the talent to make a Final Four run but the perennial inconsistency to flame out in March. The real tell is that 2.2-point move on modest volume: sharp money just got interested, and if Tommy Lloyd can stabilize the defense before tournament play, this could be the year the talent actually translates to a title.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's March Window Just Opened. Smart Money Is Already In.
daily_take.deck: Arizona jumped 2.2 points overnight to 34.8% despite zero news. This isn't random noise, it's forward-looking positioning ahead of the 2025-26 season. The market is pricing in a team that's quietly assembling NBA-level talent and plays in the weakest power conference in years. At 34.8%, you're getting a Final Four contender at near-parity odds with Illinois. The play: Arizona is underpriced relative to the talent gap widening in the Pac-12's new era.
